data.title: Dollar Wobbles As Fragile US-Iran Ceasefire Keeps Markets On Edge
data.description:
The dollar remained on a shaky footing on Thursday after broad losses, as investors anxiously assessed whether a fragile two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran would hold.

The ceasefire deal appeared to be on thin ice, as Israel continued its parallel war against the Iran-aligned militia Hezbollah in Lebanon, while Tehran accused both Israel and the U.S. of violating the agreement and said that proceeding with peace talks would be “unreasonable.”

The Strait of Hormuz also remained shut to vessels sailing without a permit and shippers said they needed more clarity before resuming transit, ticking oil prices higher. 

“There are probably some doubts emerging over whether the ceasefire expectations can really be sustained — or whether a ceasefire can even be finalized in the first place,” said Sho Suzuki, market analyst at Matsui Securities.

The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro,wobbled to be down 0.01 per cent at 99.05. The euro was up 0.01 per cent at $1.1663 while sterling edged 0.01 per cent higher to $1.3393.

The yen handed back some of the previous day’s gains following the announcement of the truce in the Middle East conflict,  weakening 0.13 per cent against the greenback to 158.8 per dollar. 

“With the Middle East situation becoming more prolonged, there seems to be a view that fiscal policy could turn more expansionary again. That, in turn, is contributing to yen weakness,” Suzuki said.

The overnight indexed swap (OIS) market is pricing in a 55 per cent chance of an interest rate hike in the Bank of Japan’s upcoming meeting later this month, data from money market broker Tokyo Tanshi showed. If the ceasefire were to collapse, expectations for the April rate increase could start to decline, which could in turn lead to yen weakness, Suzuki said.  

“The rate hike really depends on the situation in Iran, so the BOJ probably wants to wait until the very last minute before the meeting to assess how things develop,” Suzuki said. 

BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda is expected to appear in the parliament from 0415 GMT on Thursday. 

The U.S. dollar has been the major beneficiary of the Iran War among currencies, in part because the U.S. is a net energy exporter and therefore less exposed to the economic hit that oil importers like Japan and many European countries might face.

The five-week war has shaken investor confidence, triggering the largest disruption to global oil and gas supplies on record. 

The uneasy truce leaves Iran with greater leverage over shipping through the vital strait than before the conflict, analysts say, after President Donald Trump backed off from his threats to attack Iran’s civilian infrastructure.

The U.S. is set to release February personal spending and the PCE deflator on Thursday. Despite improved sentiment following the ceasefire agreement, the dollar-yen pair may remain range-bound in Tokyo trading, although strong U.S. data could trigger a rebound in the dollar, Akihiko Yokoo, senior analyst at Mitsubishi UFJ Bank, said in a note.

The Australian dollar weakened 0.13 per cent versus the greenback to $0.7033. New Zealand’s kiwi strengthened 0.07 per cent versus the greenback to $0.5826.

In cryptocurrencies,  bitcoin fell 0.61 per cent to $70,944.20. Ethereum declined 1.35 per cent to $2,180.21.

Reuters

data.title: Tokyo Stocks End Lower On Profit Taking, As Ceasefire Caution Sets In
data.description:
Tokyo stocks fell on Thursday as investors opted to lock in gains following the previous day’s surge, while caution persisted over whether the ceasefire between the United States and Iran would hold.

The benchmark Nikkei stock index, the 225-issue Nikkei Stock Average, ended down 413.10 points, or 0.73 percent, from Wednesday at 55,895.32. The broader Topix index finished 33.83 points, or 0.90 percent, lower at 3,741.47.

On the top-tier Prime Market, air transportation, retail and real estate issues were notable decliners.

Stocks briefly rose in early trading as overnight Wall Street gains kept sentiment upbeat, but profit-taking to lock in gains gradually took the upper hand.

Analysts said upward momentum was limited as investors stayed on the sidelines to see developments in the ceasefire ahead of peace talks between the United States and Iran scheduled to begin later this week in Islamabad.

data.title: Bursa Slips At Midday As West Asia Tensions Offset Wall Street Gains
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia retreated at midday on April 9, tracking cautious sentiment as renewed uncertainty in West Asia overshadowed overnight gains on Wall Street.

At 12:30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) fell 12.75 points, or 0.75%, to 1,683.56 from its close of 1,696.31 on April 8.

Across the broader market, the FBM Emas shed 78.60 points (0.63%) to 12,334.33, the FBM 70 slipped 52.30 points (0.3%) to 17,136.42, the FBM Shariah declined 39.29 points (0.32%) to 12,202.35 and the F4GBM eased 6.62 points (0.66%) to 995.63.

Market breadth was negative, with losers outpacing gainers 596 to 311, while 483 counters were unchanged, 1,318 untraded and 12 suspended.

Active trading was led by Zetrix AI Bhd, Top Glove Corporation Bhd and Borneo Oil Bhd, with Top Glove among the few bright spots, rising 5.5 sen to 79 sen. Bumi Armada Bhd also edged higher, gaining one sen to 36.5 sen, while VS Industry Bhd slipped half a sen to 19 sen.

On the broader board, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd topped the gainers list, up 20 sen to RM5.79, followed by United Plantations Bhd and Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd. Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd also advanced 16 sen to RM2.10.

Meanwhile, Nestlé (M) Bhd led decliners, sliding RM1.30 to RM99.70, with Petronas Dagangan Bhd down RM1.12 to RM20.86 and PPB Group Bhd losing 30 sen to RM11.90.

The weaker tone comes as investors remain wary of geopolitical developments, with stalled negotiations in West Asia dampening risk appetite despite earlier optimism from a ceasefire agreement.

data.title: HSIF Still In Bearish Mode, RHB
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its bearish outlook on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) following fresh negative price action.

The index fell 229 points to close at 25,719 points on Thursday after trading as low as 25,658 points during the session. It later rebounded in the evening session, gaining 181 points to last trade at 25,900 points.

RHB Research said the latest decline reinforces the view that resistance levels remain firm in the current bearish environment, while support levels are weak. The research house expects the index to trend lower once the current sideways movement completes, as bearish momentum continues to build.

Immediate resistance levels are seen at 26,000 points and 26,600 points, while support levels are pegged at 24,500 points and 23,500 points.

The research house advised traders to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage downside risks.

data.title: Stocks Slip As Middle East Truce Worries Push Oil Toward US$100
data.description:
Global share markets fell on Thursday as cracks emerged in the fragile Gulf ceasefire, driving oil prices back toward $100 a barrel and raising concerns over longer-term inflation.

Iran’s control over the Strait of Hormuz remained in focus, with reports that the vital shipping route had yet to reopen meaningfully and tolls were being demanded for safe passage. Meanwhile, Israel conducted its heaviest strikes on Lebanon since conflict began with Iran-backed Hezbollah, killing more than 250 people on Wednesday.

Brent crude rose 2.5% to $97.28 a barrel, while U.S. WTI futures gained 3.3% to $97.55. European shares dipped, with the STOXX 600 falling 0.2% after a 3.7% surge the previous day following the ceasefire announcement.

Asian markets also weakened overnight, with Japan’s Nikkei down 0.7%, South Korea falling 1.6%, and China’s CSI300 slipping 0.6%. Wall Street futures were off around 0.4% ahead of the U.S. core price data release.

The surge in energy prices is expected to feed into global inflation, with U.S. core prices for February projected to rise 0.4% before accounting for recent energy cost increases. State Street’s PriceStats indicated March saw the largest month-on-month jump in prices since at least 2008.

Federal Reserve minutes showed growing concern over inflation, though officials suggested rates may remain on hold this year. Yields on U.S. 10-year Treasury notes rose to 4.296%, while the dollar index stood at 99.06, with the euro at $1.1660.

Gold inched back to $4,713 an ounce, while European natural gas prices rebounded to 45.65 euros per megawatt hour.

Reuters

data.title: Bursa Slips At Close As Ceasefire Optimism Fades
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended lower on April 9, tracking softer regional markets as fading optimism over a United States-Iran ceasefire dampened investor sentiment.

The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) fell 10.07 points, or 0.59%, to 1,686.24 at the close, reversing much of the prior session’s relief rally as investors questioned the durability of the truce.

Broader market performance was mixed, with the FBM Emas declining 54.05 points (0.44%) to 12,358.88 and the FBM ACE easing 4.57 points (0.46%) to 997.68, while the FBM 70 edged up 9.63 points (0.06%) and the FBM Shariah gained 16.50 points (0.13%) to 12,258.14.

Turnover moderated to 3.05 billion shares worth RM2.83 billion compared to 3.77 billion shares valued at RM3.64 billion previously, reflecting a more cautious trading environment.

Among heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd dragged on the index, sliding 16 sen to RM11.16, while Public Bank Bhd lost 10 sen to RM4.60 and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd fell 11 sen to RM7.45. Gains in Tenaga Nasional Bhd, which added six sen to RM14.12, and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd, up 23 sen to RM5.82, helped cushion the decline, alongside a modest rise in IHH Healthcare.

Trading activity remained centred on lower-priced and actively traded counters, led by Borneo Oil Bhd, Zetrix AI Bhd and Bumi Armada Bhd. Top Glove Corporation Bhd was among the notable gainers in the active list, climbing 5.5 sen to 79 sen, while VS Industry Bhd slipped half a sen to 19 sen.

The weaker close underscores lingering market caution as geopolitical uncertainties continue to cloud near-term direction despite intermittent bouts of optimism.

data.title: Decliners Dominate Bursa As Selling Pressure Caps Trading Session
data.description:
The market movement ended on a weaker note, with decliners decisively outpacing gainers as investor sentiment remained cautious amid lingering market uncertainties.

Losers trounced gainers 651 to 430 at the close, while 540 counters were unchanged, 1,087 untraded and 12 suspended, underscoring the broadly negative market breadth.

Consumer heavyweights led the decline, with Nestlé (M) Bhd falling 90 sen to RM100.10, Petronas Dagangan Bhd sliding 58 sen to RM21.40 and PPB Group Bhd shedding 34 sen to RM11.86. Ayer Holdings Bhd and Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd also featured among the top losers, down 35 sen and 32 sen, respectively.

On the upside, plantation and energy-linked counters provided some support. United Plantations Bhd topped the gainers list, rising 42 sen to RM33.06, followed by Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd up 24 sen to RM22.20. Apollo Food Holdings Bhd, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd and Petronas Gas Bhd also posted modest gains.

Despite pockets of buying interest, the overall market tone remained subdued, with selling pressure across key sectors keeping Bursa firmly in negative territory at the close.

data.title: Wall Street Ends Higher As Middle East Peace Talks Lift Sentiment
data.description:
U.S. stocks advanced on Thursday, as ongoing negotiations toward a ‌peaceful resolution to the six-week Middle East conflict helped ease worries over the fragile U.S.-Iran truce.

All three major U.S. stock indexes gained ground after rebounding from an earlier dip as Israel sought talks with Lebanon.

“There’s an increasing recognition that this administration is more bark than bite, and willing to make grand ​statements about world destruction,” said Oliver Pursche, senior vice president at Wealthspire Advisors, in New York. “From an investor’s perspective, you ​have to decide whether to ignore it all or just cash in and sit it out ⁠the next two years.”

In two sessions, the S&P 500 has moved back above its 100-day and 200-day moving averages, two key ​technical levels.

Crude prices seesawed throughout the session as markets waited for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, through which one-fifth of ​the world’s oil is shipped. Front-month WTI futures settled up 3.66%, but remained below $100 per barrel.

The CBOE Market Volatility Index (.VIX), opens new tab, called the “fear index,” dipped to its lowest point since the onset of the war.

The Commerce Department issued its GDP and PCE reports, which showed the economy grew at a slower-than-expected pace ​in the fourth quarter, while consumer prices remain elevated.

Minutes from the U.S. Federal Reserve’s most recent monetary meeting showed policymakers are ​increasingly eyeing potential interest rate hikes to counter the inflationary impact of a prolonged Iran war.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 275.88 points, or ‌0.58%, to ⁠48,185.80, the S&P 500 gained 41.85 points, or 0.62%, to 6,824.66 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 187.42 points, or 0.83%, to 22,822.42.

Energy Sector Drops Most

Of the 11 major sectors in the S&P 500, energy shares dropped the most, while consumer discretionary registered the biggest percentage gain.

Consumer discretionary stocks got a boost after Amazon.com’s CEO Andy Jassy said its artificial intelligence services at its cloud-computing unit are generating ​annualized revenue of more than $15 ​billion. Amazon’s shares rose 5.6%.

Software ⁠stocks were clear underperformers on the day, sliding 2.2%, while retail and chips outperformed, rising 4.1% and 2.1%, respectively.

Constellation Brands jumped 8.5% after the company posted a smaller-than-expected drop in fourth-quarter sales.

Applied Digital shares ​dropped 8% after the data center operator’s third-quarter net loss widened from a year earlier.

Advancing issues ​outnumbered decliners by ⁠a 1.99-to-1 ratio on the NYSE. There were 254 new highs and 83 new lows on the NYSE.

On the Nasdaq, 2,651 stocks rose and 2,029 fell as advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.31-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 44 new 52-week highs and 19 new lows ⁠while the ​Nasdaq Composite recorded 158 new highs and 135 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges ​was 17 billion shares, compared with the 19.38 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Picks: QL Resources And Kossan Rubber
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified QL Resources Bhd and Kossan Rubber Industries Bhd as stocks to watch, citing bullish technical signals and potential upside momentum.

QL Resources is seen extending its gains after breaking above the RM3.95 resistance level on strong trading volume. The research house said the breakout signals strengthening bullish momentum, with the counter likely to climb towards RM4.20 and RM4.45.

However, it noted that a fall below the RM3.70 support level would negate the bullish outlook and could lead to a resumption of downward movement.

Kossan Rubber Industries is also showing signs of a potential breakout, supported by heightened trading activity and a series of higher lows above the 21-day simple moving average.

RHB Research said a move past the RM1.22 resistance level could trigger further upside towards RM1.31 and RM1.40. On the downside, a break below RM1.13 may signal the start of a correction.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 9, 2026
data.description:

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Ringgit Gains On Softer Dollar And Easing Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
The ringgit opened stronger against the US dollar on Wednesday, buoyed by improved market sentiment following a pause in geopolitical tensions after Donald Trump announced a two-week halt in military action against Iran, Bernama reported.

At 8 am, the local currency rose 0.67 per cent to 3.9975/4.0050 against the greenback, compared with Tuesday’s close of 4.0275/4.0320.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the move effectively represented a “double-sided ceasefire”, which could help ease market concerns in the near term.

“Perhaps that could provide some temporary respite to market sentiments amid heightened economic uncertainties following the shock from the war in Iran,” he told Bernama.

At the time of writing, Brent crude prices had fallen 15.70 per cent to US$92.12 per barrel, while the US Dollar Index (DXY) declined 0.35 per cent to 99.635 points, reflecting a softer demand for the greenback.

At the opening, the ringgit traded mixed against a basket of major currencies.

It strengthened against the Japanese yen to 2.5187/5238 from 2.5229/5258 at Tuesday’s close. Still, it depreciated against the euro to 4.6719/4.6806 from 4.6566/4.6618 and eased versus the British pound to 5.3570/5.3671 from 5.3425/5.3484.

The local currency traded mostly higher against ASEAN currencies.

It rose against the Singapore dollar to 3.1331/3.1392 from 3.1381/3.1419 at Tuesday’s close, appreciated against the Indonesian rupiah to 233.6/234.2 from 235.4/235.8, and strengthened against the Philippine peso to 6.63/6.64 from 6.68/6.69 previously. However, it edged lower against the Thai baht to 12.4572/12.4879 from 12.3809/12.4021.

data.title: SGX Opens Higher On Broad-Based Buying
data.description:
The Straits Times Index (STI) opened higher, rising 26.89 points, or 0.54%, to 4,984.90 as investors responded to broad-based buying across sectors and renewed optimism in regional markets.

At 10:44 am, 398 stocks advanced while 154 declined on trading volume of 931.16 million shares worth S$989.70 million. Key indices tracked the positive momentum, with the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index at 1,466.43 and the iEdge S-REIT Index at 1,037.56.

Sustainability-focused and dividend-oriented benchmarks also showed early strength, including the iEdge-OCBC Singapore Low Carbon Select 40 Capped Index at 3,616.69 and the iEdge APAC Financials Dividend Plus Index at 3,839.22.

Regional tech and emerging market indices were supported as well, with the iEdge Emerging Asia Select 50 Index at 1,406.13 and the iEdge Southeast Asia+ Tech Index at 4,404.79.

Analysts said early gains reflected positive sentiment across both blue-chip and mid-cap counters, as investors continued to monitor global developments and regional economic cues. The robust volume and wide advance-decline ratio pointed to selective but broad participation in the market.

Market watchers noted that ongoing geopolitical and economic developments, including energy market movements and regional financial indicators, could contribute to choppy trading conditions in the coming sessions.

Asian peers were mixed, with Japan’s Nikkei 225 and South Korea’s Kospi showing moderate gains amid improving investor confidence.

data.title: Bursa Rises At Midday With Select Counters Leading Gains
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia moved higher at midday, with the benchmark FBMKLCI climbing 16.32 points, or 0.97%, to 1,693.18.

Broader market indices also strengthened, as FBM70 surged 215.83 points to 17,165.07, F4GBM added 10.22 points to 1,000.38, FBMEMAS rose 129.09 points to 12,390.34, and FBMSHA gained 64.02 points to 12,208.03.

Trading was dominated by small-cap activity. AirAsia X Bhd led volume with over 60.2 million shares exchanged, gaining 0.080 to 1.240.

Other active counters included ZETRIX AI with 47.4 million shares (+0.015 to 0.755), VS Industry Bhd at 38.09 million lots (+0.005 to 0.190), Capital A with 38.9 million shares (+0.030 to 0.460), and Top Glove Corporation at 45.1 million shares (-0.030 to 0.745).

On the gainers’ board, buying interest was seen in AirAsia X Bhd, Capital A, and ZETRIX AI, while Top Glove Corporation bucked the trend with a decline. VS Industry Bhd recorded marginal gains.

Analysts noted that selective buying in highly liquid stocks supported the early gains, while investors continued to monitor market breadth and external economic developments for cues on sentiment in the afternoon session.

data.title: Bursa Sees Select Gainers Drive Early Rally As Plantation Stocks Lag
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia saw selective buying today, with consumer and energy stocks leading the gains while plantation and chemical counters retreated.

On the gainers’ board, Nestlé topped the list, rising RM2.70 to RM101.00 on moderate volume of 44,000 shares. Petronas Dagangan added RM1.02 to close at RM21.98, with 819,300 shares traded. Other active advancers included Fraser & Neave, up RM0.70 to RM29.40, Hong Leong Bank gaining RM0.60 to RM22.10, and Dutch Lady Bhd rising RM0.50 to RM32.20.

Decliners were led by United Plantations, which slipped RM1.22 to RM32.64 on 622,600 shares. Bumiputra-Kawan eased 36 sen to RM21.42, while Petronas Chemicals fell 29 sen to RM5.59 on heavy volume of 44,140,900 shares. Hibiscus Petroleum declined 25 sen to RM1.94, and Kuala Lumpur Kepong slipped 22 sen to RM21.96 on 1,009,200 shares.

Analysts noted that the session reflected selective investor interest, with buying concentrated in consumer staples and energy counters. Meanwhile, profit-taking and selling pressure in plantation and chemical stocks weighed on broader market sentiment, underscoring cautious investor behaviour heading into the next trading session.

data.title: Gold Rebound But Profit Taking Signals Caution, Says RHB
data.description:
Gold prices continued their upward rebound, with COMEX gold closing above a key technical level, although analysts warn that emerging profit-taking signals could limit further gains in the near term.

In a note, RHB said the precious metal settled at US$4,777.20 after climbing past its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), indicating improving short-term momentum. The session saw gold trade between a low of US$4,720.60 and a high of US$4,888, after opening at US$4,747.20.

Despite the stronger close, the formation of a “long upper shadow” candlestick suggests that selling pressure has begun to surface, pointing to profit-taking activity among investors.

RHB noted that if selling intensifies, gold prices could slip back below the 20-day SMA, potentially resuming a downward trend toward the US$4,600 support level.

“While the recent rebound is encouraging, the broader technical setup remains cautious unless prices break decisively above key resistance,” the research house said.

A sustained move above the US$5,000 mark would invalidate the current bearish outlook and pave the way for further upside, it added.

For now, RHB maintained its negative trading bias, advising traders to hold short positions initiated earlier, with a stop-loss set at US$5,000 to manage risks.

Key support levels are seen at US$4,600, followed by US$4,400, while resistance is pegged at US$5,000 and subsequently at US$5,250.

data.title: Bursa Ends Positive Session As Major Indices Move Up
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed higher today, supported by broad-based buying across major indices.

The benchmark FBMKLCI rose 19.45 points, or 1.16%, to 1,696.31.

Broader market indices also ended in positive territory, with the FBM70 advancing 239.48 points (+1.41%) to 17,188.72, while the FBMEMAS gained 151.68 points (+1.24%) to 12,412.93. The FBMSHA added 97.63 points (+0.80%) to 12,241.64, and the F4GBM index climbed 12.09 points (+1.22%) to 1,002.25.

Several actively traded stocks recorded gains, reflecting selective investor interest. AirAsia X Bhd led activity, rising 9 sen to RM1.25 with 87.3 million shares exchanged. VS Industry Bhd added 1 sen to RM0.195 on 72.6 million shares, while ZETRIX AI gained 2 sen to RM0.760 with 66.4 million shares traded.

Not all active counters were in the green. Velesto slipped 0.5 sen to RM0.340 on 63.9 million shares, and Top Glove fell 4 sen to RM0.735 on 57.5 million shares.

Market observers said the session reflected continued confidence in small-cap and liquid counters, while selective profit-taking in certain stocks tempered broader gains. Investor sentiment remains watchful ahead of upcoming corporate earnings and external economic developments.

data.title: Stock Picks: Malakoff And NationGate
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Malakoff Corp Bhd and NationGate Holdings Bhd as its latest trading ideas, citing improving technical momentum and bullish chart setups.

RHB Research said Malakoff is attempting a bullish breakout after testing its immediate resistance level, supported by strong trading volume and a bullish candlestick formation. The research house said a break above the RM0.85 resistance could trigger fresh buying interest, paving the way for the stock to move towards RM0.91 and potentially RM0.97.

However, it noted that a decline below the RM0.79 support level could weaken sentiment and place the stock back into a correction phase.

For NationGate, RHB Research said the counter appears to be resuming its upward movement after staging a bullish breakout. The stock closed above its RM0.73 resistance level with a bullish candlestick, signalling that a positive setup remains intact.

The research house added that the counter has stayed above its 21-day simple moving average (SMA), indicating that the near-term trend remains constructive. If momentum continues, NationGate could advance towards RM0.82, followed by RM0.91.

On the downside, RHB Research said a fall below the RM0.64 support level would invalidate the bullish view.

The note comes as traders continue to scan for short-term opportunities amid cautious broader market sentiment.

data.title: HSIF Roars Higher, Still Not Enough For RHB To Turn Bullish
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) maintained its short trading call on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) even as the contract extended its rebound and approached a key technical breakout zone.

RHB Research said the HSIF surged 860 points in the previous session to close at 25,948 points, after opening at 25,849 points, hitting an intraday high of 26,107 points and a low of 24,689 points. At the time of writing, the contract had added a further 112 points to 26,060 points.

The research house said the strong move, marked by a long bullish candlestick, suggests momentum is picking up, with the index now poised to move above its 50-day simple moving average (SMA). It added that the relative strength index (RSI) has also crossed above the 50% level, indicating improving near-term sentiment.

Still, RHB Research said it is retaining its negative bias for now and advised traders to hold on to the short position initiated at 26,367 points on Feb 26, unless the HSIF closes above the 26,600-point stop-loss level.

It noted that a sustained close above 26,000 points in the coming sessions would confirm a bullish breakout and could improve overall market sentiment.

For now, 24,500 points remains the initial support level, followed by 23,500 points, while immediate resistance is seen at 26,000 points ahead of 26,600 points.

data.title: Wall Street Ends Sharply Higher On US-Iran Ceasefire
data.description:
U.S. stocks closed sharply higher on Wednesday after a last-minute, two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran lifted investor sentiment.

All three ‌major U.S. stock indexes surged at the opening bell, muscled higher by a broad relief rally after a deal brokered by Pakistan resulted in a two-week suspension of the war. The conflict, which began with joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28, has sent world markets reeling, disrupted global oil supply and sparked fears of rising inflation.

A senior Iranian official told Reuters that the crucial Strait ​of Hormuz, through which one-fifth of the world’s oil is shipped, could be reopened on Thursday or Friday ahead of peace talks if the countries agreed ​upon a framework for the ceasefire.

“It’s an expected move today and there’s still a lot of work to do, but I ⁠think the market is quite relieved,” said Mike Dickson, head of portfolio management at Horizon Investments in Charlotte, North Carolina. “The other side of this coin could have been ​a lot worse and frankly there’s a good reason to think that it was possible too. So you’re seeing that relief rally in the hardest-hit areas of the market.”

The ​S&P 500 shot above its 200-day moving average for the first time since mid-March, while the Dow registered its largest single-session percentage gain since April 9, 2025.

Economically sensitive Dow Transports touched an all-time high, while the Russell 2000 outperformed its larger-cap peers. Chips jumped 6.3%.

The rally was not confined to U.S. indexes. European shares rose 3.9%, while MSCI’s World index was up over 3%. Both indexes logged ​their biggest one-day percentage gains in a year.

“Most other countries were more exposed to an energy shock and a food shock than the U.S.,” said Ross Mayfield, investment ​strategy analyst at Baird in Louisville, Kentucky. “So this is a much bigger near-term relief for international stocks.”

The CBOE Market Volatility index, a barometer of investor anxiety, dipped to its lowest level ‌since the beginning ⁠of the war.

Front-month WTI and Brent crude futures fell 16.4% and 13.3%, respectively, both settling below $100 per barrel.

Minutes from the U.S. Federal Reserve’s March meeting, released on Wednesday, showed a growing openness to rate hikes as policymakers raised their 2026 inflation outlook due to war-related oil shock.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 1,326.33 points, or 2.85%, to 47,910.79, the S&P 500 gained 165.98 points, or 2.51%, to 6,782.83 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 617.15 points, or 2.80%, to 22,635.00.

Of the 11 major sectors in the S&P 500, eight ​jumped 2% or more, with industrials leading ​the pack. Energy stocks, dragged down ⁠by falling crude prices, were the sole percentage losers, dropping 3.7%.

Sectors that have suffered a beating since the war began enjoyed a robust bounceback. Commercial airlines jumped 5.7%, travel and leisure-related stocks shot up 5.2% and homebuilders rose 4.9%.

Delta Air Lines gained 3.8%, ​despite its disappointing second-quarter profit forecast. The commercial air carrier declined to update its annual outlook due to uncertainties related to ​the Iran war.

Delta peers Southwest ⁠Airlines and United Airlines advanced 6.7% and 7.9%, respectively.

Cruise operator Carnival added 11.2% and Norwegian Cruise Line rose by 7.6%.

Levi Strauss jumped 10.7% after the apparel maker raised its annual sales and profit forecasts.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 5.67-to-1 ratio on the NYSE. There were 197 new highs and 45 new lows on the NYSE.

On the Nasdaq, 3,582 stocks rose ⁠and 1,174 fell ​as advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 3.05-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 21 new 52-week highs ​and 5 new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 133 new highs and 57 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 20.64 billion shares, compared with the 19.42 billion average for the full session over the last ​20 trading days.

Reuters
